detaileddescription.para: Maximum length of a single command line (including null terminator).
Maximum number of arguments (including the command name).
Adds framework-aware commands to the CLI. Each is individually guarded by a define (default: all enabled). The commands operate on the framework singletons you pass in.

cli
CLI instance.
Built-in commands (enable/disable via defines):
version — print syn_version() info (SYN_CLI_CMD_VERSION, default 1)
uptime — print tick_ms uptime (SYN_CLI_CMD_UPTIME, default 1)
errors — dump errlog entries (SYN_CLI_CMD_ERRORS, default 1)
tasks — show scheduler task states (SYN_CLI_CMD_TASKS, default 1)

parameterdescription.para: Argument vector. argv[0] is the command name.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or an application-defined error code.

memberdef.argsstring: (SYN_CLI *cli, const SYN_CLI_Command *commands, size_t cmd_count, const char *prompt)
memberdef.name: syn_cli_init

detaileddescription.para: Output goes directly to syn_port_serial_write — no callback needed.

parameterdescription.para: Prompt string (e.g., "> " or "syntropic> "). Stored by pointer, not copied.

detaileddescription.para: When enabled (default), the CLI echoes each received character back to the terminal. Disable if the terminal handles its own echo.

parameterdescription.para: true to enable echo, false to disable.

detaileddescription.para: Call this for each byte received from the input stream (UART RX ISR, polled read, etc.). The CLI handles line editing (backspace) and dispatches the command when a newline is received.
Alternative to process_char when you already have a full line (e.g., from a buffered read). Does not echo or handle editing.
Called automatically after command execution. Can also be called manually (e.g., on startup to show the initial prompt).

briefdescription.para: Print formatted output through the CLI's output function.

detaileddescription.para: Convenience function for command handlers that need printf-style output.

briefdescription.para: Set errlog instance for the errors built-in command.

parameterdescription.para: Error log instance (or NULL to detach).

briefdescription.para: Set scheduler instance for the tasks built-in command.

detaileddescription.para: Provides a lightweight interactive shell over UART (or any byte stream). Commands are registered as a static table — no dynamic allocation. Input is processed one character at a time, making it easy to feed from a UART ISR or polled loop.

listitem.para: Automatic argc/argv parsing (whitespace-delimited, with quoted strings)
Echo control (for terminals that don't echo locally)
